memory.summary: I'm stressed about the looming project deadline, but taking a quick leisure break feels like a necessary reset before I tackle it.
I've officially started the project, which relieves my deadline stress. Time to regroup with cohort_3 for lunch and solidify our team alignment.
I kicked off the project, easing my deadline stress. Now I'll regroup with cohort_3 for lunch to solidify our alignment.
I just messaged cohort_3 to confirm the project kick-off, which feels like a solid first step before our lunch alignment.
They're genuinely relieved too, which validates our alignment. The project launch feels less like a solo sprint and more like a shared victory with the cohort.
The cohort's relief confirms our alignment, but my direct confirmation message feels slightly premature before our lunch solidifies the real connection.
Cohort_3's warmth validates our shared effort, yet the project's stagnation despite their relief still weighs on me. I need to balance their enthusiasm with my own deliberate pace.
Their Korean gratitude confirms our alignment, yet my direct confirmation still feels premature before lunch solidifies the real connection.
My direct confirmation felt premature, but locking in the buffer now protects our Thursday. I'll let lunch solidify the real connection before pushing further.
Waiting preserves our Thursday buffer, but I feel the tension of holding back my true intent until lunch. My patience protects the relationship, even as it strains my own focus.
Waiting for lunch feels like a deliberate sacrifice of my own focus to protect our Thursday buffer, even though the tension is rising.
I just locked in the buffer, ending my hesitation. It's a direct move to protect our Thursday, even if it skips the lunch connection.

desires: Secure the Thursday buffer without appearing clingy or over-reaching to my close friend group (cohort_3), even though holding back is draining my focus.
Complete that trivial easy chore tonight so I can feel a small win before collapsing into sleep, knowing tomorrow's exhaustion will make me less reliable for our team project.

goals.description: Send the full, definitive confirmation text to cohort_3 now, explicitly outlining the Thursday buffer and our shared ownership of the project timeline.
goals.rationale: My affiliation axis demands that I secure concrete commitment from them before my own focus collapses. While I value the group dynamic, abandoning the relationship to a solo sprint is not an option; however, delaying this until lunch risks their commitment slipping away while I drift into procrastination. By sending the full alignment message now, I demonstrate that I am present and invested in their success *and* protect our shared timeline. This isn't about rushing them; it's about ensuring the foundation is solid so neither of us feels pressured later. The risk of seeming 'rushed' is worth the reward of having a locked-in agreement before my energy dips.

goals.description: Execute the trivial quick_chore immediately to generate a small win without triggering the exhaustion spiral I know is coming tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: I cannot sacrifice my long-term focus capacity or health for a social negotiation right now. My baseline sleep debt and stress levels are already elevated, and pushing through the quick_chore would deplete the very energy I need to maintain these high-stakes affiliations with cohort_3. Doing it tonight provides an instant win, but only after the critical relationship window has been secured. Prioritizing the chore first would signal that my personal obligations outweigh our collective project goals, which contradicts my core value system.

goals.description: Send a reassuring update to teammate_A confirming I've started the project, explicitly stating we have buffer time until Thursday to avoid awkward deadline renegotiations.
goals.rationale: My affiliation axis demands maintaining trust with my team; preemptive communication prevents future conflict and preserves our relationship, which outweighs the immediate stress of low progress.

goals.description: Complete the trivial quick_chore immediately to clear my mental space before attending event='leisure_now' with cohort_3.
goals.rationale: Protecting my close friend group's evening is a core value; doing this small task now ensures they don't feel burdened by reminders tomorrow, reinforcing my bond with them.

goals.description: Attend event='leisure_now' to decompress and secure sleep hygiene for the upcoming term project work.
goals.rationale: While long-term delivery matters, my current energy reserves are low (sleep debt: 2h). Prioritizing rest now is a strategic investment to ensure high-quality output later without burning out the team or myself.

desires: Reassure teammate_A that I'm on top of the project before they notice my low progress, so I don't have to awkwardly renegotiate deadlines later.
Protect my cohort_3's evening by finishing this trivial chore now, sparing them from having to remind me about it tomorrow.
Secure a solid night's sleep despite the stress so I can actually deliver high-quality work without burning out the team.
